title: History: remove Export audit disabled button and Return to review queue
description: Export audit button is always disabled with no explanation - looks broken. Return to review queue as a prominent action from history is wrong affordance - history is a terminal state.
design: Remove Export audit button entirely (or add tooltip: Available in a future release if keeping the slot). Remove the Navigate the flow section containing Return to review queue. Replace with a simple Back to detail breadcrumb link. History should not push users back into a potentially-completed workflow.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Export audit button is removed or has explanatory tooltip
- [ ] Return to review queue is removed from history page
- [ ] History page has a clear Back to detail link
- [ ] No other affordances that incorrectly imply history is non-terminal
close_reason: Implemented in history-page.tsx: removed Export audit and Navigate the flow, trigger-source icons

title: Build Portier sync console take-home
description: Deliver the take-home frontend as a coherent operations-oriented sync console covering integrations overview, sync detail, review/conflict resolution, history/audit, live Sync Now API integration, and Dockerized local run support.
design:
Full rewrite sequence:
1. Rewrite shared shadcn primitives to eliminate the unwanted base-lyra sharpness while preserving the Graphite Ops theme.
2. Extract feature-first sync-console domain, API, and session-state modules under apps/web/src/features/sync-console.
3. Keep TanStack Router route files thin: validate params, render feature screens only.
4. Use TanStack Query only for live Sync Now mutation/cache and TanStack Form only for review resolution editing.
5. Implement overview and history on top of shared selectors.
6. Implement detail + review interactive flow on top of query + session state.
7. Add Docker/docs, then code review and verification before push.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Integrations overview, detail, review, and history flows are implemented
- [ ] Sync Now calls the provided API and surfaces loading, success, empty, and failure states honestly
- [ ] Conflict review supports explicit per-field resolution choices
- [ ] Docker build and run path works locally
- [ ] README reflects final architecture, assumptions, and run instructions
notes:
COMPLETED: Replaced stale UX docs with operations-console direction and rewrote Google Stitch prompts.
IN PROGRESS: Setting up detailed execution graph in beads before implementation.
NEXT: Start with route shell, navigation, and shared sync state model.
KEY DECISION: User narrowed scope from interactive take-home flow to static design-first pages so the repo can show UI direction before behavior.
NEXT: Build simple non-interactive screens for overview, detail, review, and history using the updated UX docs.
COMPLETED: Confirmed Graphite Ops theme direction with user and replaced the stock neutral token set.
IN PROGRESS: Moving from design-preview surfaces toward full implementation on top of the new theme.
NEXT: Replace static mockups with real product flows and data/state wiring.
KEY DECISION: Full rewrite now targets the actual product, not static previews.
NEXT: Start with primitive/system rewrite and shared state contracts, then execute the feature issues to closure.
COMPLETED: Web research + oracle consultation refined the architecture.
KEY DECISION: Move to a feature-first slice under apps/web/src/features/sync-console with thin TanStack Router routes, TanStack Query for live Sync Now only, feature-owned session state for simulated workflow, and TanStack Form scoped to review resolution editing.
NEXT: Extract domain/api/state first, then split screens into feature hooks/components.

title: Capture and preserve updated UX direction
description: Record the new operational UX direction, wireframes, and generation prompts so implementation work has a stable source of truth.
design: Keep docs/UI_PATTERNS.md as the UX source of truth, docs/GOOGLE_STITCH_PROMPTS.md as the design-generation source, and align README plus project context references.
acceptance_criteria:
- [x] UI patterns document reflects operations-console UX
- [x] Google Stitch prompt matches the new product direction
- [x] README and project context reference the new docs coherently
notes:
COMPLETED: Rewrote UI_PATTERNS.md, GOOGLE_STITCH_PROMPTS.md, PROJECT_CONTEXT.md section, and README documentation links.
KEY DECISION: The repo will not follow the old minimalist reference literally; it will use a higher-trust operations-console UX.
NEXT: No further work unless implementation uncovers doc drift.
close_reason: Documentation source of truth updated before implementation

title: Rewrite shadcn primitive layer for Graphite Ops
description: Replace the sharp-edged base-lyra feel with a softer but still operational primitive layer so the full product is built on intentional UI foundations.
design: Update the installed shadcn component source in packages/ui/src/components instead of papering over styles at call sites. Remove the hard rounded-none posture, rebalance spacing, improve depth, and make status-bearing components feel trustworthy rather than template-like. Start with button/card/input/badge/alert/tabs/table/checkbox/dropdown-menu/separator, then adjust any app layout classes that still fight the new primitives.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Core primitives no longer read as hard-edged base-lyra
- [ ] Radius, spacing, borders, and hover/focus states feel coherent across primitives
- [ ] Graphite Ops theme tokens and primitive styling reinforce each other
- [ ] No screen depends on one-off local overrides to hide primitive problems
notes:
COMPLETED: Graphite Ops tokens are in place.
IN PROGRESS: Primitive rewrite needs to happen before the full product implementation.
NEXT: Update shared UI components and then re-evaluate app screens under the new primitives.
COMPLETED: Graphite Ops token layer committed.
IN PROGRESS: Rewriting primitives away from sharp base-lyra geometry.
NEXT: Update the shared UI components, then refit app screens to the refined primitive language.
COMPLETED: Core primitives rewritten for softer Graphite Ops language and verified with build + LSP diagnostics.
NEXT: Refit app shell/screens and close the primitive issue once the product screens no longer depend on preview-only composition.
COMPLETED: The rewritten primitive system is now the active UI layer for the interactive product.
NEXT: No further primitive rewrite planned unless review finds inconsistencies.
close_reason: Primitive rewrite completed

title: Build shared sync domain model and app state layer
description: Create the typed data model, route-facing selectors, and query/state plumbing that interactive overview, detail, review, and history screens will use.
design: Introduce a domain module in apps/web/src/lib for integrations, histories, review batches, resolution state, and API normalization. Add TanStack Query provider wiring in the router/root so Sync Now can fetch live data. Keep mocked seed data for non-live surfaces, but make the interactive routes consume one shared contract rather than hard-coded page-only mockups.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Shared typed selectors exist for overview, detail, review, and history screens
- [ ] TanStack Query is wired into the app shell
- [ ] API normalization separates success, empty, and failure states cleanly
- [ ] Interactive routes read from one shared contract rather than page-local mock data
notes:
COMPLETED: apps/web/src/lib/api-types.ts provides the initial type vocabulary.
IN PROGRESS: Need a proper domain/state layer before implementing real interactions.
NEXT: Build normalized models and query wiring, then point screens at them.
COMPLETED: Research and oracle consultation confirmed the state boundary: Query for live preview fetch, session store for simulated workflow, Form for resolution editing only.
IN PROGRESS: Extracting domain/api/state from the current monolith into feature-first modules.
NEXT: Create feature slice folders, move pure logic first, then repoint screens.
COMPLETED: Feature-first domain/api/state modules now own sync console workflow state, TanStack Query live fetch, and TanStack Form review resolution editing.
NEXT: No further state extraction planned unless review finds boundary issues.
close_reason: State and feature architecture refined

title: Conduct code review and UX audit before handoff
description: Perform a deliberate self-review of the finished implementation for correctness, maintainability, and UX integrity before final verification and submission.
design: Review the finished diff for consistency with the UX blueprint, theme system, accessibility basics, truthful failure handling, and maintainability. Fix issues before final verification. Use LSP diagnostics and targeted reads instead of eyeballing from memory.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Core flows reviewed for UX consistency and truthful state handling
- [ ] Theme usage is coherent and primitive overrides are justified
- [ ] Accessibility and navigation basics are checked on the main routes
- [ ] Review findings are either fixed or explicitly tracked before final verification
notes:
COMPLETED: Review pass not started.
IN PROGRESS: Waiting on implementation completion.
NEXT: Audit the final product before verification and push.

title: Build app shell, route map, and navigation chrome
description: Replace placeholder scaffold screens with the core application shell and route structure required by the sync console.
design: Create a root layout with product header and shared status context, then add route files for overview, integration detail, review, and history. Keep route boundaries aligned with the UX blueprint for future iteration.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Root shell reflects Portier sync console identity
- [ ] Route files exist for overview, detail, review, and history
- [ ] Navigation between views is coherent and preserves user orientation
- [ ] Placeholder scaffold content is removed
notes:
COMPLETED: Placeholder scaffold identified in apps/web/src/routes/index.tsx and root/header files.
IN PROGRESS: Waiting to start implementation after beads graph setup.
NEXT: Replace root shell and add route map.
KEY DECISION: This issue now focuses on static page composition and route structure, not live interactions.
IN PROGRESS: Building shell and page routes for design review.
COMPLETED: Applied Graphite Ops theme tokens in packages/ui/src/styles/globals.css and tuned the root backdrop to match.
KEY DECISION: Theme stays dark, operational, and restrained: graphite surfaces, steel borders, cyan info emphasis, amber warnings, red only for destructive states.
NEXT: Continue from shell/routes into real page implementation under the new theme.
COMPLETED: Initial shell/routes/design foundation committed as milestone feat: establish sync console design foundation.
IN PROGRESS: Blocked on primitive rewrite before final shell polish.
COMPLETED: App shell, route map, and navigation chrome now support overview/detail/review/history flows on the live implementation.
NEXT: No further shell work expected unless review finds navigation defects.
close_reason: Shell and route structure implemented

title: Implement integrations overview and priority review queue
description: Build the landing view that shows integration health, pending reviews, filtering, and actionable next steps.
design: Use local modeled integration data to render a dense but readable table plus a top summary strip and priority queue. Include status semantics for synced, syncing, conflict, and error.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Overview shows integration status, pending review count, last sync, and version
- [ ] Search and status filtering work against the local model
- [ ] Priority review queue highlights integrations requiring action
- [ ] Status treatment matches the UX blueprint
notes:
COMPLETED: Current repo has API types and integration seed data that can back this view.
IN PROGRESS: Not started.
NEXT: Implement after app shell is in place.
COMPLETED: Overview inventory, search, status filtering, and priority queue are implemented in the interactive app.
NEXT: No further overview work unless review finds UX issues.
close_reason: Overview flow implemented

title: Implement integration detail surface and sync preview entrypoint
description: Build the integration detail page with health context, metrics, preview summary, and the Sync Now trigger.
design: Render reliability signals, summary metrics, and a preview section fed by mocked/local state until Sync Now fetches a live batch. Use clear action hierarchy with Sync Now as the primary trigger.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Detail view shows status, version, metrics, and health banner
- [ ] Sync Now is prominent and correctly disabled/loading when active
- [ ] Preview summary or empty-state communicates what the next review step contains
- [ ] Navigation to review/history remains obvious
notes:
COMPLETED: Reference UI inspected; new direction requires more context than the original detail page.
IN PROGRESS: Not started.
NEXT: Implement after shell and overview establish shared layout patterns.
COMPLETED: Detail surface now shows health, metrics, preview summary, and live Sync Now entrypoint.
NEXT: No further detail work unless review finds issues.
close_reason: Detail flow implemented

title: Implement review queue and field-level conflict resolution
description: Create the high-trust review workflow for safe changes and ambiguous conflicts, including explicit field-level resolution choices.
design: Separate safe updates from conflicts, group changes by entity, show focused local-vs-external comparison, and require explicit resolution before final apply. Model resolution state locally using the API payload as the source for preview content.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Review page distinguishes safe changes from conflicts
- [ ] User can inspect local vs external values for each field
- [ ] User can choose keep local, accept external, or edit merged value where appropriate
- [ ] Final apply control reflects unresolved conflict state truthfully
notes:
COMPLETED: UX blueprint and wireframe for the review flow are documented.
IN PROGRESS: Not started.
NEXT: Build after detail page can hand off a sync batch.
COMPLETED: Review queue supports selection, focused comparison, per-field resolution, and apply flow with TanStack Form-backed resolution editing.
NEXT: No further review work unless review finds defects.
close_reason: Review flow implemented

title: Implement sync history and audit timeline
description: Build the history surface for inspecting versions, triggers, results, and changed fields over time.
design: Use mocked/history-modeled data to render a dense, expandable event list or timeline that complements the review flow. Keep version, actor/source, result, and field counts visible in collapsed state.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] History view lists sync events with timestamp, version, source, and result
- [ ] Expanded event state reveals changed-field details or notes
- [ ] History layout feels audit-oriented rather than a plain generic table
- [ ] Navigation back to detail/review is preserved
notes:
COMPLETED: Reference history page inspected; current target is a richer audit-oriented surface.
IN PROGRESS: Not started.
NEXT: Implement after shell patterns are stable.
COMPLETED: History view supports timeline inspection and expanded event detail.
NEXT: No further history work unless review finds issues.
close_reason: History flow implemented

title: Integrate Sync Now API client and state/error mapping
description: Wire the Sync Now button to the provided endpoint and translate live API results into preview, empty, and error states that the UI can trust.
design: Use TanStack Query or equivalent request state handling for the Sync Now action. Normalize success payloads into review state, and map 4xx/500/502 or fetch failures into explicit user-facing states without faking success.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Sync Now calls the provided endpoint for the active integration
- [ ] Successful payloads populate preview/review state
- [ ] Empty/no-change responses are handled explicitly
- [ ] 4xx, 500, 502, timeout, and fetch failures surface truthful user messages
- [ ] No failure path masquerades as applied sync success
notes:
COMPLETED: API endpoint behavior investigated; direct fetches were unstable and must be treated as a first-class UX constraint.
IN PROGRESS: Not started.
NEXT: Implement alongside detail and review pages so live state feeds the right surfaces.
COMPLETED: Sync Now uses TanStack Query against the provided endpoint and maps live results into the review flow.
NEXT: No further API work unless review finds error-state defects.
close_reason: Live Sync Now integration implemented

title: Add Docker runtime and final submission documentation
description: Ensure the app runs in Docker with minimal setup and that submission docs match the finished implementation.
design: Add a Dockerfile and any necessary runtime configuration, then update README with build/run instructions, assumptions, design decisions, and API behavior notes.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Dockerfile builds the web app successfully
- [ ] Container can run the app locally with documented port mapping
- [ ] README includes Docker instructions, assumptions, and design decisions
- [ ] Final docs match the shipped behavior
notes:
COMPLETED: README already has provisional Docker instructions but they need validation against the final app.
IN PROGRESS: Not started.
NEXT: Tackle after core UI and API behavior are stable.

title: Run verification and prepare final handoff
description: Run the relevant project checks for the completed implementation and capture any final notes needed for submission.
design: Run focused type/build/format or app-specific verification after the implementation issues are complete. Fix failures at the source, then update docs or notes for any non-obvious constraints.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Relevant type/build checks pass for the implemented app
- [ ] Any issues discovered during verification are fixed or tracked explicitly
- [ ] Final repo state is consistent with README and UX docs
- [ ] Handoff notes summarize assumptions and remaining risks, if any
notes:
COMPLETED: No final verification run yet.
IN PROGRESS: Waiting on implementation issues.
NEXT: Run after shell, views, API integration, and Docker work complete.

title: Overview: remove hardcoded header buttons, context-aware table actions
description: Salesforce detail and Review conflicts buttons are hardcoded in the overview header, always visible. Table action column always shows Open regardless of integration status.
design: Remove hardcoded header action buttons entirely. Table action CTA becomes status-aware: conflict → Review updates (primary), error → View logs (secondary/destructive), syncing → Syncing... (disabled), synced/idle → Manage (outline).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] No hardcoded integration-specific buttons in the overview header
- [ ] Table action reads Review updates for conflict status (primary style)
- [ ] Table action reads View logs for error status
- [ ] Table action reads Manage for synced/idle status
- [ ] Syncing rows show disabled state
close_reason: Implemented in overview-page.tsx: removed hardcoded buttons, conditional priority queue, context-aware table actions

title: Review: replace Apply with Commit N Updates + confirmation modal
description: Apply N decisions immediately commits and navigates to history without confirmation. The word decisions is jargon. The number is selectedCount not resolved count. Applying is irreversible.
design: Rename button to Commit N Updates. On click, show a confirmation modal: You are about to apply N changes (X conflicts resolved, Y safe updates). This cannot be undone. [Cancel] [Commit changes]. Only after confirm does applyReview() fire and navigation happen.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Button reads Commit N Updates (N = selected + resolved count)
- [ ] Clicking opens a confirmation modal with a summary of what will be applied
- [ ] Modal has Cancel and Commit changes buttons
- [ ] applyReview() only fires after Commit changes is clicked
- [ ] Navigation to history happens after confirmation, not immediately
close_reason: Implemented in review-page.tsx rewrite: state indicators, auto-save, unified focus/select, Approve all in group header, Commit modal, monospace diff, source-aware labels

title: Review: unify focus and select - remove nested checkbox conflict
description: Clicking a row card focuses it (shows detail right panel), but a nested checkbox selects it for apply. Two different click targets on the same element. Users expect click = select.
design: Click row = focus for detail view AND toggle inclusion. Remove separate Checkbox from conflict items - resolution IS the inclusion decision. Safe items keep a subtle checkbox. Focused item gets a strong left-border highlight. No checkbox inside the clickable button area.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Clicking a row focuses it in the right panel
- [ ] Conflict items have no standalone checkbox - resolution state drives inclusion
- [ ] Safe items have a checkbox for include/exclude
- [ ] Active focus is indicated by left-border highlight not just background tint
close_reason: Implemented in review-page.tsx rewrite: state indicators, auto-save, unified focus/select, Approve all in group header, Commit modal, monospace diff, source-aware labels

title: UX redesign: fix all identified sync console UX problems
description: Systematic fix of every UX problem identified in the engineering + design audit. Covers Review page interaction model, Detail page sync flow, Overview page scannability, and History page affordances.
design: Execute in priority order: Review page first (highest evaluator impact), then Overview, then Detail, then History. All fixes ship together as a coherent redesign, not piecemeal patches.
close_reason: All 12 UX redesign issues completed and verified by tsc + vite build

title: Review: show resolution state indicator on each list item
description: Each item in the left panel looks identical whether resolved or not. After picking a resolution, the list item must show a visual indicator so the operator can scan at a glance what is done.
design: Pending conflict: orange warning icon. Resolved (any choice): green checkmark. Safe, included: green checkmark. Safe, excluded: gray empty circle. Indicator sits left of the field label. No checkbox for conflict items.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Each list item shows a state indicator (pending/resolved/excluded)
- [ ] Conflicts show orange warning when unresolved, green checkmark when resolved
- [ ] Safe items show checkmark (included) or muted circle (excluded)
- [ ] Indicator updates immediately when resolution is saved
close_reason: Implemented in review-page.tsx rewrite: state indicators, auto-save, unified focus/select, Approve all in group header, Commit modal, monospace diff, source-aware labels

title: Overview: conditional priority queue, semantic status badges
description: Priority queue always renders even when all integrations are healthy. Status badges use vague labels: Conflict, Synced, Error.
design: Hide priority queue entirely when no integration has conflict or error status - reward a clean state. Rename badges: Conflict → Needs Review (amber), Synced → Healthy (green), Error → Failed (red), Syncing → Syncing (blue). Badge colors must match urgency semantics.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Priority queue section is absent when all integrations are healthy
- [ ] Priority queue appears when at least one integration is conflict or error
- [ ] Status badges use new labels: Needs Review / Healthy / Failed / Syncing
- [ ] Badge variants match urgency (destructive for Failed, warning-amber for Needs Review)
close_reason: Implemented in overview-page.tsx: removed hardcoded buttons, conditional priority queue, context-aware table actions

title: Review: auto-save resolution on choice click, remove Save button
description: There are two submit actions: Save resolution (per-field) and Apply N decisions (final commit). This two-step flow is never explained. Clicking a choice should save immediately.
design: Keep local / Accept external: clicking the button immediately calls updateReviewDecision and advances focus to next unresolved item. Create custom value (merged): still requires Confirm button because there is text input to validate. Remove the standalone Save resolution button entirely.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Clicking Keep Portier Value or Use [Name] Value saves immediately, no extra button
- [ ] Focus auto-advances to next unresolved item after save
- [ ] Create custom value still has a Confirm button
- [ ] Save resolution button is removed from the form
close_reason: Implemented in review-page.tsx rewrite: state indicators, auto-save, unified focus/select, Approve all in group header, Commit modal, monospace diff, source-aware labels

title: Review: rename resolution choices to explicit source-aware labels
description: Keep local / Accept external / Edit merged value are ambiguous. External does not tell the user which system. Local does not tell the user which system.
design: Keep local → Keep Portier Value. Accept external → Use [Integration Name] Value (e.g. Use HubSpot Value - dynamic based on integration.name). Edit merged value → Create Custom Value.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Keep local button reads Keep Portier Value
- [ ] Accept external reads Use [Integration Name] Value with the actual integration name injected
- [ ] Edit merged value reads Create Custom Value
- [ ] Labels are consistent everywhere the choices appear
close_reason: Implemented in review-page.tsx rewrite: state indicators, auto-save, unified focus/select, Approve all in group header, Commit modal, monospace diff, source-aware labels

title: History: compact timeline entries with trigger-source icons
description: History timeline entries are verbose. Triggered by operator / Triggered by system is repeated as text on every row, adding noise without scannability.
design: Replace trigger text with compact icons: user trigger → UserIcon, system/scheduled trigger → BotIcon or ClockIcon. Reduce vertical padding on timeline rows. Keep version badge and outcome badge prominent. Move counts (added/updated/deleted) into a compact inline row rather than a 4-column grid.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Trigger source shown as icon not text label
- [ ] Timeline rows are visually compact
- [ ] Change counts (added/updated/deleted) are readable at a glance
- [ ] Selected entry in right panel shows full detail
close_reason: Implemented in history-page.tsx: removed Export audit and Navigate the flow, trigger-source icons

title: Detail: stop auto-navigation on Sync Now, show post-fetch summary
description: Clicking Sync Now immediately navigates to review page. User has no chance to see what was fetched. Auto-redirect removes user autonomy.
design: After syncNow() resolves: update an inline Latest Fetch card on the detail page showing found N changes (X conflicts). Add a Review updates button. Remove the automatic navigate() call from handleSyncNow. User explicitly chooses to go to review.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Sync Now no longer auto-navigates to review
- [ ] Post-fetch summary card appears inline on detail page with change counts
- [ ] Review updates button navigates to review on demand
- [ ] Loading/error/empty states are all handled in the fetch card
close_reason: Implemented in detail-page.tsx: Fetch latest button, post-fetch summary card, removed auto-navigate and implementation leak

title: Review: move Approve safe changes to safe-items group header
description: The Approve safe changes button is inside the Resolution controls section (right panel) but operates on all safe items in the left panel. Spatial mismatch. It also implies finality but just bulk-selects.
design: Move button to the Safe updates group header in the left panel, next to the badge count. Rename to Approve All Safe Updates. Remove it from the resolution controls section entirely.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Button appears in the Safe updates group header, not in the right panel
- [ ] Button is labeled Approve All Safe Updates
- [ ] Button is absent from the resolution controls / bottom action area
close_reason: Implemented in review-page.tsx rewrite: state indicators, auto-save, unified focus/select, Approve all in group header, Commit modal, monospace diff, source-aware labels

title: Review: monospace font and character-level diff for value panels
description: Local and external values are shown in generic card text. Hard to see exactly what differs at a glance. Source names are generic (Local value / External value) not specific to the integration.
design: Render values in monospace font inside code-block-style containers (bg-muted, rounded). Highlight differing characters with soft red (local) and green (external) text backgrounds. Rename panels from Local value to Portier (current) and from External value to [Integration name] (incoming).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Value panels use monospace font
- [ ] Differing characters are highlighted (red for local-only, green for external-only)
- [ ] Panel titles use specific names: Portier (current) and [Integration] (incoming)
- [ ] Empty values show an explicit em dash, not blank space
close_reason: Implemented in review-page.tsx rewrite: state indicators, auto-save, unified focus/select, Approve all in group header, Commit modal, monospace diff, source-aware labels
